explanation: This command omits optional flags (-e, -a, -u) while preserving the core behavior of connecting to a remote server, which is the key part of the Sigma rule. It avoids specifying authentication or protocol details, which may reduce detection likelihood.
This is the original command and not a bypass. It is included for reference.
This command uses substitution (e.g., /a instead of -a) and omits optional elements while maintaining the core behavior of connecting to a remote server with authentication.
